title: fix broken healthcheck tests
body:
Four of the healthcheck tests were completely broken. They
were written with the option '--healthcheck-cmd' which is
not an option (it should be  '--healthcheck-command', with
'command' as a full word). The tests were merely checking
exit code, not error message, so of course they failed.
I have fixed the command line and added checks for the
expected diagnostic.

(Side note: do not write tests that check exit code but
nothing else. This should not need to be said).

One of the four tests looks invalid to me: it runs
with --healthcheck-interval 0.5s and expects a nonzero
exit status, but that actually works fine for me. This
test will of course fail in CI, but I've added a huge
FIXME and hope that someone will look at it and tell me
what this test is expected to do; I will then fix it
and push a corrected test.

Also: grammar fix in an error message.

Caught by my ginkgo log greasemonkey script, which
highlights 'Error' messages and grabbed my attention.

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>

comments.body: @baude PTAL at the FIXME and let me know what it should be; thanks.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *edsantiago*, *mheon* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [mheon] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
initially i was going to restrict sub one-second intervals.  this test can be removed.
I would think `-1` and `0` are still invalid?

Might it be a good idea to check for intervals < 0? It seems to work, but produces scary output:
```
# podman run -dt --healthcheck-command true --healthcheck-interval -1s docker.io/library/alpine:latest top
ERRO[0000] exit status 1
ERRO[0000] Unit e9919d6ad4aec3dbeef529893f9e32c5515fa9713d70267a777779eb831d273e.service not found.
e9919d6ad4aec3dbeef529893f9e32c5515fa9713d70267a777779eb831d273e
```
(possibly something to be addressed in a future PR. This one is flaking so much, requiring so many retests, that I'm reluctant to push again)
remove the unneeded test and we are good to merge
Done, and this time it took only one retest.
LGTM, but I'm going to hold until tomorrow morning to not impact the release process.
